text.title: Refactoring Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 hard rules every refactor pass must obey.

rule.statement: Each commit applies exactly one transform from the closed catalog.
rule.rationale: Bisect / revert needs atomic units; multi-transform commits make both impossible.

rule.statement: Tests are run between every move; the next move is blocked until green.
rule.rationale: Compounding moves under red tests cascade.

rule.statement: A refactor PR makes no behaviour change. New behaviour ships in a separate PR.
rule.rationale: Mixing refactor + behaviour confuses review and bisect.

rule.statement: Commit message names the transform exactly: 'extract-function: ...', 'inline-variable: ...'.
rule.rationale: Searchable history; reviewers know what to verify.

rule.statement: Renames use the IDE's / LSP's symbol-rename, never grep-replace.
rule.rationale: grep-replace misses shadowed scopes and matches in comments; the LSP scope analyser does both correctly.
